jobDistinction.legacyPausedAuditJob: Broad website issue discovery plus small automatic fixes/upgrades; currently paused.
jobDistinction.currentRemediationJob: Backlog-driven remediation, optimization, and upgrade loop based on .hermes/remediation-backlog.md.

nonOverlapPolicy: If the previous remediation run is still active, skip the current tick instead of starting another fix run.

freshAuditPolicy.action: Run comprehensive audit, update remediation-backlog.md with newly discovered or reprioritized issues, then continue remediation.

lastHealthyBaseline.tests: Full Vitest passed 172 files / 693 tests during fresh audit checkpoint
lastHealthyBaseline.production: vcontrolhub-next and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws active; /api/status healthy; major public HTML/API probes returned 307/401/200; recent service logs clean

lastRun.target: Fresh comprehensive audit checkpoint after remediation counter threshold
lastRun.summary: Ran threshold-triggered comprehensive health/audit checkpoint with no code changes required. Quality gates, full Vitest, build/runtime build, production route probes, smoke test, and recent log checks passed. Backlog updated with remaining P6 durable-worker architecture and smaller newly observed audit candidates.
validation: git status checked and clean before audit
production services active and /api/status healthy before audit
source scans for API guard coverage, plain API forms, reload usage, internal anchors, bounded query candidates, and shell execution candidates
production probes returned expected 307/401/200 statuses without 500
recent production logs checked with no error/warn matches
lastRun.deployment: No runtime/code change in this audit checkpoint; services were already active and smoke-tested successfully, so no restart was required.
lastRun.nextTarget: Continue P6 durable command/deployment background-worker architecture: restart-surviving queue claims/worker ownership/richer task reconciliation; alternatively address smaller audit candidates if a deployable slice is safer.

runPolicy.batchSize: coherent deployable batch; may be larger for high-value architecture/performance/availability/user-experience improvements if fully verified
